name: Documentation & public API surface
outcome: The OIDC layer is fully documented and every new public type is importable from the package entry point.
scope: Confirm public exports, update CLAUDE.md, and add an OIDC page to the docs site. Documentation and wiring only.
acceptance_criteria: All new OIDC public types are importable from the package entry point
CLAUDE.md describes the OIDC layer in the architecture section
The docs site has an OIDC page covering key setup, the oidc config block, per-adapter endpoint wiring, and the claims hooks
The obligation to persist nonce and auth_time for opaque codes is documented
The one-time migration consequence is documented: enabling OIDC switches access tokens from HS256 to RS256/ES256 and invalidates access tokens issued before the switch
steps: index.ts — confirm all new OIDC public types (option types, ID token and claims types, discovery types) are exported
CLAUDE.md — document the OIDC layer in the architecture section

docs site — add an OIDC page covering key setup, the oidc config block, endpoint wiring per adapter, getUserClaims and getIdTokenClaims, the obligation to persist nonce and auth_time for opaque codes, and the one-time access-token migration when OIDC is enabled (HS256 to RS256/ES256, invalidating previously issued access tokens)
docs site — verify the page builds and links from the navigation
notes: Final integration slice. The adapters need no code changes — the three OIDC endpoints return a plain ResponseInterface every adapter already handles, so the docs only show wiring. ADRs 0001 and 0002 and CONTEXT.md already capture the rationale and glossary.
